title: Greater London Council Staff Association

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British trade union

Greater London Council Staff Association

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Merged into | [GMB](/wiki/GMB_(trade_union) "GMB (trade union)") |
| Founded | 1909 |
| Dissolved | 14 September 1988[[1]](#cite_note-marsh-1) |
| Headquarters | 150 [Waterloo Road, London](/wiki/Waterloo_Road,_London "Waterloo Road, London") |
| Location | * England |
| Members | 17,059 (1982)[[2]](#cite_note-tuh82-2) |
| Publication | *Greater London Staff Association Bulletin*[[2]](#cite_note-tuh82-2) |
| Affiliations | [TUC](/wiki/Trades_Union_Congress "Trades Union Congress") |

The **Greater London Council Staff Association** (GLCSA) was a [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") representing workers for the [London](/wiki/London "London")-wide council.

The union was founded in 1909 as the **London County Council Staff Association**, to represent clerical, technical and professional staff at the [London County Council](/wiki/London_County_Council "London County Council"). It was initially a section of the [National Association of Local Government Officers](/wiki/National_Association_of_Local_Government_Officers "National Association of Local Government Officers") (NALGO), but in 1916 it split from NALGO and became independent. It held a majority of trade union seats on the [Whitley Council](/wiki/Whitley_Council "Whitley Council") for London staff, although membership always remained below 20,000. While NALGO repeatedly tried to get the union to re-affiliate, association members feared that if it did so, the London Whitley Council would be merged into the national one for local authority workers.

In 1955, the union affiliated to the [Trades Union Congress](/wiki/Trades_Union_Congress "Trades Union Congress"), ten years before NALGO followed suit.[[3]](#cite_note-waddington-3) When the London County Council was replaced by the [Greater London Council](/wiki/Greater_London_Council "Greater London Council"), the union changed its name to the **Greater London Council Staff Association**. In later years, it also represented workers at the [Inner London Education Authority](/wiki/Inner_London_Education_Authority "Inner London Education Authority"), [London Ambulance Service](/wiki/London_Ambulance_Service "London Ambulance Service"), [Thames Water Authority](/wiki/Thames_Water_Authority "Thames Water Authority"), and at [polytechnics](/wiki/Polytechnic_(United_Kingdom) "Polytechnic (United Kingdom)") and probation services in the city.[[2]](#cite_note-tuh82-2)

In 1985, the union shortened its name to the **Greater London Staff Association** (GLSA).[[1]](#cite_note-marsh-1) The Greater London Council was abolished in 1986, greatly reducing the union's membership. As a result, in 1988 it merged into the [GMB](/wiki/GMB_(trade_union) "GMB (trade union)").[[1]](#cite_note-marsh-1)[[4]](#cite_note-British-4)

## General Secretaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Greater_London_Council_Staff_Association&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: General Secretaries")]

:   1955: Laurence Welsh
:   1963: Frederick Thomas Hollocks
:   1980s: C. S. Corcoran
:   1986: Arthur Capelin
